main_paper.raw: {"methods":["Mathematical decomposition of hypothesis discovery into three subtasks: inspiration retrieval, hypothesis composition, and hypothesis ranking","MOOSE-CHEM: an agentic LLM framework implementing the decomposition","Evolutionary algorithm for associating background and inspirations","Multi-step inspiration retrieval and composition","Efficient ranking method for generated hypotheses"],"problems":["Can LLMs autonomously generate novel and valid chemistry hypotheses given only a research background?","How to decompose the intractable task of hypothesis discovery into practical subtasks?","How to evaluate LLMs' ability to rediscover unseen chemistry hypotheses without data contamination?"],"datasets":["Benchmark of 51 high-impact chemistry papers (published in Nature, Science, or similar in 2024), each annotated with background, inspirations, and hypothesis by PhD chemists"],"metrics":["Similarity between generated and ground-truth hypotheses","Accuracy of inspiration retrieval","Ranking quality of hypotheses"],"results":[{"metric":"Hypothesis rediscovery (similarity to ground truth)","value":"MOOSE-CHEM can rediscover many hypotheses with very high similarity to the ground truth, covering the main innovations"},{"metric":"Inspiration retrieval accuracy","value":"LLMs show surprisingly high accuracy on retrieving relevant inspirations, even for out-of-distribution tasks"}],"novelty_claims":["First mathematical derivation for decomposing hypothesis discovery into practical subtasks in chemistry","First agentic LLM framework (MOOSE-CHEM) for rediscovering unseen chemistry hypotheses, using evolutionary algorithms and multi-step inspiration retrieval","First benchmark of 51 high-impact, post-2024 chemistry papers annotated for hypothesis discovery evaluation","Demonstration that LLMs can rediscover main innovations of recent Nature/Science chemistry papers without data contamination","Proposal that LLMs may encode latent scientific knowledge associations not yet recognized by humans"]}

methods: Mathematical decomposition of hypothesis discovery into three subtasks: inspiration retrieval, hypothesis composition, and hypothesis ranking
MOOSE-CHEM: an agentic LLM framework implementing the decomposition
Evolutionary algorithm for associating background and inspirations
Multi-step inspiration retrieval and composition
Efficient ranking method for generated hypotheses
problems: Can LLMs autonomously generate novel and valid chemistry hypotheses given only a research background?
How to decompose the intractable task of hypothesis discovery into practical subtasks?
How to evaluate LLMs' ability to rediscover unseen chemistry hypotheses without data contamination?
datasets: Benchmark of 51 high-impact chemistry papers (published in Nature, Science, or similar in 2024), each annotated with background, inspirations, and hypothesis by PhD chemists
metrics: Similarity between generated and ground-truth hypotheses

results.value: MOOSE-CHEM can rediscover many hypotheses with very high similarity to the ground truth, covering the main innovations
LLMs show surprisingly high accuracy on retrieving relevant inspirations, even for out-of-distribution tasks

novelty_claims: First mathematical derivation for decomposing hypothesis discovery into practical subtasks in chemistry
First agentic LLM framework (MOOSE-CHEM) for rediscovering unseen chemistry hypotheses, using evolutionary algorithms and multi-step inspiration retrieval
First benchmark of 51 high-impact, post-2024 chemistry papers annotated for hypothesis discovery evaluation
Demonstration that LLMs can rediscover main innovations of recent Nature/Science chemistry papers without data contamination
Proposal that LLMs may encode latent scientific knowledge associations not yet recognized by humans

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Monte Carlo Reasoner (MCR): a Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS)-based approach for prompting LLMs to perform combinatorial search and complex scientific reasoning","Use of domain-specific reward functions (e.g., adsorption energy) to guide LLM query refinement","Tree-structured prompt decomposition to explore combinations of catalyst properties in LLM queries"],"problems":["Combinatorial explosion in catalyst discovery due to multiple chemical properties and trade-offs","Limitations of LLMs in scientific reasoning, including brittleness to input prompts and hallucination","Need for goal-driven, combinatorial search using LLMs for scientific discovery, specifically in catalyst design","Improving specificity and scientific value of LLM-generated answers in chemistry"],"datasets":["BioFuels Question Reasoning (BioFuelQR): a new dataset of complex reasoning questions and answers curated with catalysis researchers","A curated dataset of computational chemistry simulations"],"metrics":["Improvement over best baseline (percentage)","Specificity and quality of LLM answers (qualitative and quantitative analysis)"],"results":[{"metric":"Improvement over best baseline","value":"25.8%"}],"novelty_claims":["First use of Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) to guide LLM prompting for combinatorial scientific reasoning in catalyst design","Introduction of BioFuelQR, a new dataset capturing complex reasoning challenges in catalysis","Demonstration that domain-specific reward functions (e.g., adsorption energy) can significantly improve the specificity and quality of LLM answers in scientific tasks","Improvement over state-of-the-art chain-of-thought prompting variants for scientific reasoning"]}

methods: Monte Carlo Reasoner (MCR): a Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS)-based approach for prompting LLMs to perform combinatorial search and complex scientific reasoning
Use of domain-specific reward functions (e.g., adsorption energy) to guide LLM query refinement
Tree-structured prompt decomposition to explore combinations of catalyst properties in LLM queries
problems: Combinatorial explosion in catalyst discovery due to multiple chemical properties and trade-offs
Limitations of LLMs in scientific reasoning, including brittleness to input prompts and hallucination
Need for goal-driven, combinatorial search using LLMs for scientific discovery, specifically in catalyst design
Improving specificity and scientific value of LLM-generated answers in chemistry
datasets: BioFuels Question Reasoning (BioFuelQR): a new dataset of complex reasoning questions and answers curated with catalysis researchers
A curated dataset of computational chemistry simulations
metrics: Specificity and quality of LLM answers (qualitative and quantitative analysis)
novelty_claims: First use of Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) to guide LLM prompting for combinatorial scientific reasoning in catalyst design
Introduction of BioFuelQR, a new dataset capturing complex reasoning challenges in catalysis
Demonstration that domain-specific reward functions (e.g., adsorption energy) can significantly improve the specificity and quality of LLM answers in scientific tasks
Improvement over state-of-the-art chain-of-thought prompting variants for scientific reasoning

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Multi-module framework called MOOSE based on large language model (LLM) prompting","Three feedback mechanisms: present-feedback, past-feedback, and future-feedback to improve hypothesis generation"],"problems":["Automated open-domain scientific hypothesis discovery (TOMATO task)","Generating valid, novel, and helpful scientific hypotheses from raw web corpus","Overcoming limitations of prior work that used handpicked, close-domain observations and commonsense hypotheses"],"datasets":["TOMATO dataset: constructed from 50 recent social science papers (post-Jan 2023) with associated raw web corpus (news, Wikipedia, business reviews)"],"metrics":["GPT-4 based evaluation","Expert-based evaluation (by social science researchers)"],"results":[{"metric":"GPT-4 evaluation (relative to LLM baseline)","value":"MOOSE performs better than LLM baseline"},{"metric":"Expert evaluation (novelty and validity)","value":"Many MOOSE-generated hypotheses recognized as both novel and valid by social science researchers"}],"novelty_claims":["First dataset and task setting for open-domain, automated scientific hypothesis discovery using raw web corpus (TOMATO)","First demonstration that LLMs can generate novel (not existing in literature) and valid (reflecting reality) scientific hypotheses","Introduction of a multi-module LLM-based framework (MOOSE) with three novel feedback mechanisms for hypothesis generation"]}

methods: Multi-module framework called MOOSE based on large language model (LLM) prompting
Three feedback mechanisms: present-feedback, past-feedback, and future-feedback to improve hypothesis generation
problems: Automated open-domain scientific hypothesis discovery (TOMATO task)
Generating valid, novel, and helpful scientific hypotheses from raw web corpus
Overcoming limitations of prior work that used handpicked, close-domain observations and commonsense hypotheses
datasets: TOMATO dataset: constructed from 50 recent social science papers (post-Jan 2023) with associated raw web corpus (news, Wikipedia, business reviews)

results.value: MOOSE performs better than LLM baseline
Many MOOSE-generated hypotheses recognized as both novel and valid by social science researchers

novelty_claims: First dataset and task setting for open-domain, automated scientific hypothesis discovery using raw web corpus (TOMATO)
First demonstration that LLMs can generate novel (not existing in literature) and valid (reflecting reality) scientific hypotheses
Introduction of a multi-module LLM-based framework (MOOSE) with three novel feedback mechanisms for hypothesis generation

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["SciMON framework: a modeling framework that retrieves 'inspirations' from past scientific papers and iteratively optimizes for novelty by comparing generated ideas to prior work and updating them until sufficient novelty is achieved","Automated data collection methodology: collects examples of past problems and proposed ideas from scientific papers for fine-tuning and in-context training of LLMs","In-context contrastive model: encourages novelty with respect to background context by contrasting generated ideas with existing literature"],"problems":["Limited expressivity in literature-based hypothesis generation due to focus on binary link prediction","Lack of explicit optimization for novelty in hypothesis generation","Inability of current LLMs (e.g., GPT-4) to generate novel and technically deep scientific ideas","Need for models that can generate natural language suggestions for novel scientific directions grounded in literature"],"datasets":["Automatically collected dataset of past problems and proposed ideas from scientific papers (AI/NLP and biomedical domains)"],"metrics":["Human expert evaluation: relevance, utility, novelty, technical depth"],"results":[{"metric":"Novelty (human evaluation)","value":"SciMON methods substantially improve novelty of generated ideas compared to GPT-4 baseline"},{"metric":"Technical depth (human evaluation)","value":"SciMON methods partially mitigate the low technical depth of GPT-4, but still fall short of scientific papers"},{"metric":"Relevance and utility (human evaluation)","value":"SciMON methods improve over GPT-4, but generated ideas still lag behind actual scientific papers"}],"novelty_claims":["First framework to generate natural language scientific ideas grounded in literature, using background context rather than binary link prediction","Introduction of an iterative novelty optimization process for idea generation, where generated ideas are compared to prior work and updated for increased novelty","Development of an in-context contrastive model to explicitly encourage novelty in generated ideas","First comprehensive evaluation of language models for generating scientific ideas in a generative, contextual setting"]}

methods: SciMON framework: a modeling framework that retrieves 'inspirations' from past scientific papers and iteratively optimizes for novelty by comparing generated ideas to prior work and updating them until sufficient novelty is achieved
Automated data collection methodology: collects examples of past problems and proposed ideas from scientific papers for fine-tuning and in-context training of LLMs
In-context contrastive model: encourages novelty with respect to background context by contrasting generated ideas with existing literature
problems: Limited expressivity in literature-based hypothesis generation due to focus on binary link prediction
Lack of explicit optimization for novelty in hypothesis generation
Inability of current LLMs (e.g., GPT-4) to generate novel and technically deep scientific ideas
Need for models that can generate natural language suggestions for novel scientific directions grounded in literature
datasets: Automatically collected dataset of past problems and proposed ideas from scientific papers (AI/NLP and biomedical domains)
metrics: Human expert evaluation: relevance, utility, novelty, technical depth

results.value: SciMON methods substantially improve novelty of generated ideas compared to GPT-4 baseline
SciMON methods partially mitigate the low technical depth of GPT-4, but still fall short of scientific papers
SciMON methods improve over GPT-4, but generated ideas still lag behind actual scientific papers

novelty_claims: First framework to generate natural language scientific ideas grounded in literature, using background context rather than binary link prediction
Introduction of an iterative novelty optimization process for idea generation, where generated ideas are compared to prior work and updated for increased novelty
Development of an in-context contrastive model to explicitly encourage novelty in generated ideas
First comprehensive evaluation of language models for generating scientific ideas in a generative, contextual setting

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["ResearchAgent: an LLM-powered system for iterative research idea generation over scientific literature","Augmentation with an academic graph to connect related publications","Entity-centric knowledge store derived from co-occurrences of key concepts across papers","LLM-powered ReviewingAgents that provide iterative feedback and reviews, aligned with human preference criteria elicited via LLM prompting","Iterative refinement of research ideas through feedback loops between ResearchAgent and ReviewingAgents"],"problems":["Slow and effort-intensive process of generating novel research ideas due to the overwhelming volume of scientific literature","Difficulty in synthesizing knowledge and identifying cross-domain connections for impactful research","Lack of automated systems to assist in the ideation phase (problem identification, method proposal, experiment design) of scientific research"],"datasets":["Scientific publications across multiple disciplines (exact datasets not specified in the provided content)"],"metrics":["Human evaluation (clarity, relevance, significance, novelty, validity)","Model-based evaluation"],"results":[{"metric":"Human/model evaluation (clarity, relevance, significance, novelty, validity)","value":"ResearchAgent outperforms strong LLM-powered baselines by large margins in generating more clear, relevant, significant, and especially novel ideas"}],"novelty_claims":["First work to leverage and evaluate LLMs as mediators in open-ended scientific idea generation, rather than just experimental validation","Introduction of an entity-centric knowledge store to enhance cross-domain idea generation","Use of LLM-powered ReviewingAgents with evaluation criteria aligned to real human researcher judgments for iterative idea refinement","Comprehensive modeling of the human research workflow (literature review, concept mapping, peer feedback) in an automated system"]}

methods: ResearchAgent: an LLM-powered system for iterative research idea generation over scientific literature
Augmentation with an academic graph to connect related publications
Entity-centric knowledge store derived from co-occurrences of key concepts across papers
LLM-powered ReviewingAgents that provide iterative feedback and reviews, aligned with human preference criteria elicited via LLM prompting
Iterative refinement of research ideas through feedback loops between ResearchAgent and ReviewingAgents
problems: Slow and effort-intensive process of generating novel research ideas due to the overwhelming volume of scientific literature
Difficulty in synthesizing knowledge and identifying cross-domain connections for impactful research
Lack of automated systems to assist in the ideation phase (problem identification, method proposal, experiment design) of scientific research
datasets: Scientific publications across multiple disciplines (exact datasets not specified in the provided content)
metrics: Human evaluation (clarity, relevance, significance, novelty, validity)

results.metric: Human/model evaluation (clarity, relevance, significance, novelty, validity)
results.value: ResearchAgent outperforms strong LLM-powered baselines by large margins in generating more clear, relevant, significant, and especially novel ideas

novelty_claims: First work to leverage and evaluate LLMs as mediators in open-ended scientific idea generation, rather than just experimental validation
Introduction of an entity-centric knowledge store to enhance cross-domain idea generation
Use of LLM-powered ReviewingAgents with evaluation criteria aligned to real human researcher judgments for iterative idea refinement
Comprehensive modeling of the human research workflow (literature review, concept mapping, peer feedback) in an automated system

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["The AI Scientist: a fully automated end-to-end framework for scientific discovery using frontier large language models (LLMs)","Automated pipeline including idea generation, literature search, experiment planning, code writing (using Aider), experiment execution, result visualization, manuscript writing, and automated peer review","Use of chain-of-thought and self-reflection LLM frameworks to improve decision-making","Automated reviewer based on foundation models for paper evaluation"],"problems":["Automating the entire scientific discovery process in machine learning, from ideation to publication, without human intervention","Overcoming the limitations of previous research automation approaches that constrain the search space and require substantial human expertise","Enabling open-ended, iterative scientific research and discovery by AI agents"],"datasets":["ICLR 2022 OpenReview data (for evaluating the automated reviewer)"],"metrics":["Balanced accuracy (for reviewer evaluation)","Acceptance threshold at top machine learning conferences (as judged by the automated reviewer)","Financial cost per generated $15"},{"metric":"Paper quality (acceptance threshold)","value":"AI Scientist can produce papers that exceed the acceptance threshold at a top ML conference as judged by the automated reviewer"}],"novelty_claims":["First comprehensive, fully automated, and scalable end-to-end framework for scientific discovery in machine learning using LLMs","First demonstration of an AI system that can independently generate research ideas, conduct experiments, write full scientific papers, and perform peer review without human involvement","Automated reviewer based on foundation models achieves near-human performance in evaluating scientific papers","The system can operate in an open-ended, iterative loop, building on its own discoveries, akin to the human scientific community","Demonstrates the ability to generate hundreds of medium-quality papers in a week at low cost"]}

methods: The AI Scientist: a fully automated end-to-end framework for scientific discovery using frontier large language models (LLMs)

Automated pipeline including idea generation, literature search, experiment planning, code writing (using Aider), experiment execution, result visualization, manuscript writing, and automated peer review
Use of chain-of-thought and self-reflection LLM frameworks to improve decision-making
Automated reviewer based on foundation models for paper evaluation
problems: Automating the entire scientific discovery process in machine learning, from ideation to publication, without human intervention
Overcoming the limitations of previous research automation approaches that constrain the search space and require substantial human expertise
Enabling open-ended, iterative scientific research and discovery by AI agents
datasets: ICLR 2022 OpenReview data (for evaluating the automated reviewer)
metrics: Acceptance threshold at top machine learning conferences (as judged by the automated reviewer)

results.value: 65% vs. 66% on ICLR 2022 OpenReview data
AI Scientist can produce papers that exceed the acceptance threshold at a top ML conference as judged by the automated reviewer

novelty_claims: First comprehensive, fully automated, and scalable end-to-end framework for scientific discovery in machine learning using LLMs
First demonstration of an AI system that can independently generate research ideas, conduct experiments, write full scientific papers, and perform peer review without human involvement
Automated reviewer based on foundation models achieves near-human performance in evaluating scientific papers
The system can operate in an open-ended, iterative loop, building on its own discoveries, akin to the human scientific community
Demonstrates the ability to generate hundreds of medium-quality papers in a week at low cost

methods: ChemReasoner: a framework combining heuristic search over a large language model's (LLM) knowledge space with quantum-chemical feedback from atomistic graph neural networks (GNNs)
LLM-driven hypothesis generation and planning for catalyst discovery
Automated planning methods for search space exploration without human input (ChemReasoner-Planner)
Structure-based scoring using GNNs trained on quantum chemistry simulations
Evaluation of candidate catalysts via 3D atomistic representations and scoring functions (adsorption energies, reaction energy barriers)
problems: Accelerating the discovery of new, efficient catalysts for chemical processes

Multi-modal provenance trails
700,000+ atomistic trajectories

novelty_claims: Introduction of ChemReasoner, a novel framework unifying LLM-driven heuristic search with quantum-chemical feedback for catalyst discovery

Establishment of domain-grounding for LLMs via quantum chemical property feedback, enabling reasoning beyond simple adsorption energies to include reaction pathways and energy barriers
Provision of large, open datasets and code for the community, including 700,000+ atomistic trajectories and DFT validation

novelty_claims: This is the first systematic survey dedicated to exploring the roles of LLMs across the entire scientific research process, not just specific tasks or domains.

The survey provides a holistic and integrated analysis of LLMs' contributions across hypothesis discovery, experiment planning, scientific writing, and peer review, unlike previous surveys with narrower scopes.
It identifies current challenges and proposes future research directions for LLMs in scientific research, aiming to inspire and guide further advancements.

methods: Self-consistency decoding strategy for chain-of-thought prompting
Sample-and-marginalize procedure for answer selection
problems: Improving reasoning performance of language models on complex multi-step reasoning tasks
Overcoming limitations of greedy decoding in chain-of-thought prompting
Reducing repetitiveness and local-optimality in language model outputs for reasoning tasks
datasets: GSM8K
SVAMP
AQuA
StrategyQA
ARC-challenge
novelty_claims: Proposes a novel self-consistency decoding strategy for chain-of-thought prompting that samples diverse reasoning paths and selects the most consistent answer.
Self-consistency is unsupervised, requires no additional training, human annotation, or auxiliary models, and works off-the-shelf with pre-trained language models.
Self-consistency acts as a 'self-ensemble' on a single language model, differing from traditional ensemble or verifier-based approaches.
Demonstrates significant and robust improvements over previous decoding strategies (greedy, sample-and-rank, beam search, ensemble) across multiple reasoning benchmarks.

methods: End-to-end Case-Based Reasoning Framework (ECBRF) for CKBC
Retrieval of relevant cases (knowledge tuples) from a case base to augment input
Random mask training strategy to prevent overfitting to retrieved cases

results.value: ECBRF outperforms COMET
ECBRF outperforms COMET
ECBRF outperforms COMET
ECBRF generates more valid, informative, and novel knowledge than COMET in both fully supervised and few-shot settings

novelty_claims: First to integrate the full methodology of Case-Based Reasoning (CBR) with pretrained language models (PLM) for CKBC.
Proposes an end-to-end framework (ECBRF) that retrieves and utilizes relevant cases (knowledge tuples) as non-parametric knowledge for CKBC.
Introduces a random mask training strategy to improve robustness and performance.
Demonstrates that CBR methodology can improve deep learning models, especially in few-shot settings, addressing a fundamental question in the CBR community.
Provides detailed analysis and insights on why previous retrieval-based methods did not improve over PLM baselines, and why their approach does.
